name: Senda del Oso: Valdemurio Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Valdemurio  — circular por la Senda del Oso
description: Hike the easy 2.0-mile Senda del Oso: Valdemurio Reservoir Loop, enjoying reservoir views and a tunnel in Asturias, Spain.
region_intro: Hiking around Quirós offers diverse terrain within Asturias, Spain, characterized by dramatic natural landscapes. The region features the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, with towering peaks of the Ubiña Massif and extensive ancient beech and oak woodlands. Impressive gorges, such as the Ruta de las Xanas and Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas, are carved by streams and rivers. Mountain passes like Puerto de la Cubilla provide expansive views and high mountain pastures. Best hiking trails around Quirós The most popular hiking route is Senda del Oso: Valdemurio Reservoir Loop, a 2.0 miles (3.3 km) trail that takes 53 minutes to complete, offering accessible paths around the Valdemurio Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda del Oso Greenway, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This former mining railway track provides gentle gradients through picturesque areas. Local hikers also love the Peña Rueda Circular Trail from Lindes, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through alpine landscapes with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Quirós is defined by protected natural parks, deep gorges, and high mountain passes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging alpine ascents. The routes in Quirós are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Quirós's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect in Quirós?
A: Quirós offers a diverse range of hiking experiences, from easy greenways to challenging alpine ascents. You'll find trails through lush ancient beech and oak woodlands, dramatic gorges, and high mountain pastures. There are 22 routes available, with 6 easy, 9 moderate, and 7 difficult options to choose from.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Quirós?
A: Yes, Quirós is home to the iconic Senda del Oso (Bear Path), which is particularly suitable for families due to its gentle gradients and well-maintained surface. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232128740/senda-del-oso-valdemurio-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso: Valdemurio Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3 km trail that takes less than an hour to complete and offers accessible paths around the reservoir.

Q: What natural landmarks or features can I see while hiking in Quirós?
A: Quirós is rich in natural beauty. You can explore impressive gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas on the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huerta Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features scenic mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views. Don't miss the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanga Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; for a refreshing stop.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more strenuous experience, the region offers routes with significant elevation gain through alpine landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238549483/pena-rueda-circular-trail-from-lindes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Rueda Circular Trail from Lindes&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.1 km route that typically takes nearly 7 hours to complete, leading through high mountain terrain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Quirós?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For a challenging option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238549483/pena-rueda-circular-trail-from-lindes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Rueda Circular Trail from Lindes&lt;/a&gt;. Another circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238632545/ricao-y-sierra-de-pandemineros-circular-por-bueida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricao y Sierra de Pandemineros — circular por Bueida&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 12 km hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Quirós?
A: The best time to hike in Quirós generally depends on the type of experience you're looking for. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with lush greenery in spring and stunning foliage in autumn. Summer is ideal for higher altitude treks, while some lower-altitude trails may be accessible in winter, though conditions can be challenging with snow and ice in the mountains.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Quirós?
A: The hiking trails in Quirós are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's dramatic natural landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the variety of routes catering to all skill levels.

Q: Is Quirós part of any larger natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, Quirós is nestled within the &lt;strong&gt;Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa&lt;/strong&gt;, which has been a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since 2012. This natural park is known for its towering peaks, extensive ancient forests, and unique glacial and karst formations, offering a truly protected and wild hiking environment.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking opportunities in the Quirós area?
A: Yes, Quirós is part of the &lt;strong&gt;GR 109 Asturias Interior&lt;/strong&gt;, a natural path that traverses the Asturian interior. This allows hikers to embark on longer journeys, immersing themselves in the region's natural beauty and experiencing traditional rural life over multiple days.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, Quirós features several scenic mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238650075/royal-trail-of-puerto-de-ventana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Trail of Puerto de Ventana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km hike that takes you through one of these notable passes, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Quirós using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options to every trailhead can be limited in rural Asturias, some larger towns in the Quirós area may have bus connections. For popular routes like the Senda del Oso, it's advisable to check local bus schedules to nearby villages, or consider driving and utilizing designated parking areas.

Q: Are there any trails that feature gorges or dramatic rock formations?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its impressive gorges. The well-known "Route of the Xanas" (Ruta de las Xanas) winds through a spectacular gorge carved by the Las Xanas stream, revealing fascinating stalactites. Another dramatic passage is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas&lt;/a&gt;, found along the Senda del Oso.
tips: The Valdemurio Reservoir Footbridge is a structure that allows visitors to enjoy panoramic views of the reservoir and its natural surroundings. This footbridge is part of the Senda del Oso, a very popular hiking and cycling route in Asturias.
Mandatory stop. It has a walkway that crosses the Senda del Oso. It is a very beautiful place with wonderful views. There is a bar if you fancy something (closes at 7pm).

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Oso: Valdemurio Reservoir Loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is considered easy and is highly suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families with children. The path is generally flat with minimal elevation gain, making it very accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Valdemurio Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows an old railway bed, so it's relatively flat and well-maintained. You'll encounter varied surfaces including gravel, unpaved sections, and some paved areas. The route also features a well-lit 103-meter tunnel, bridges, and passes through gallery forests and open clearings.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers wonderful views of the Valdemurio Reservoir and the surrounding mountains, including the Sierra del Aramo. A highlight is the Valdemurio Footbridge, which provides panoramic vistas. You'll also pass through picturesque 'vegetal tunnels' formed by trees and dramatic gorges.

faqs.question: Can I see wildlife, especially bears, on this trail?
faqs.answer: While you might spot local wildlife, a significant highlight along the larger Senda del Oso, of which this loop is a part, is the opportunity to see brown bears, Paca and Molina, in a dedicated enclosure. This is a popular attraction, especially for children.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Valdemurio Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the Valdemurio Reservoir area, which serves as a common starting point for the loop. This area often has recreational facilities, making it a convenient access point.

faqs.question: Is the Valdemurio Reservoir Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Senda del Oso, including the Valdemurio Reservoir Loop, is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially given the presence of other hikers, cyclists, and potential wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Valdemurio Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery with lush greenery or autumnal colors. Summer is also popular, but it can be warmer. Winter hikes are possible, but check local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the surrounding natural park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Valdemurio Reservoir Loop or to access the surrounding Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of the renowned Senda del Oso greenway. It also intersects with sections of other official routes, including Asturias Interior (Etapa 15), Sendero Histórico: Asturias, and Vía Verde Senda del Oso.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the Valdemurio Reservoir?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Valdemurio Reservoir area is a popular recreational spot. You'll find facilities for water sports, fishing, and dedicated recreation areas with picnic tables. Bike rentals are also often available for those wishing to explore more of the Senda del Oso by bicycle.

faqs.question: Are there any unique historical features or structures on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is built upon an old mining railway line, giving it a unique historical context. You'll traverse a 103-meter-long, well-lit tunnel and cross several bridges that are remnants of this industrial past, adding a distinctive character to the natural beauty.
